title: Missing nonReentrant modifier on USDT buy functions
reason: The concrete risk here is the unsafe low-level USDT payment handling itself; a separate reentrancy finding was not substantiated beyond the same misconfigured/malicious-token assumption and did not show an additional protocol-harm path.

title: Owner can change staking contract to any address
reason: As stated, this is an owner-privilege observation rather than an independent vulnerability. The reportable issue is the lack of validation that allows accidental misconfiguration to silently break staking flows, which is already captured in F-005.

title: Unlimited token approval in startClaim without revocation
reason: Changing stakingContract later does not grant the new address the old approval; the approval remains with the address approved in startClaim. The candidate's drain path is incorrect as written and reduces to trusted-admin risk.

title: Division before multiplication causes precision loss
reason: The ETH path already multiplies before dividing, and the USDT path's decimal conversion only introduces normal rounding/truncation. No realistic fund-loss or exploitable protocol-level harm was supported.

title: claimAndStake deletes userDeposits before verifying staking success
reason: If depositByPresale reverts, the whole transaction reverts and userDeposits is not deleted. The real issue is silent success against an invalid staking target, which is covered by F-005.

title: No slippage protection on token purchases
reason: This is a fixed-price sale flow, not an AMM swap. The absence of a min-out parameter is not a standalone vulnerability on the shown code paths.

title: Missing events for critical administrative functions
reason: This is observability/code-quality feedback, not a realistic protocol-harm issue.

title: No access control on updateFromBSC function
reason: The function is gated by onlyOwner. Arbitrary owner-controlled imports are a trust/centralization property, not a missing-access-control bug.

title: stakeingWhitelistStatus can be disabled after claims start
reason: This is an owner-controlled policy toggle with no unsupported access path. It does not constitute an independent vulnerability.
